ref: But you have promised that I would be a success and that someday it will be as hard to count my descendants as it is to count the grains of sand along the seashore.
text: Wo na wokaa sɛ: Yie na mɛyɛ woɔ, na mɛma w’asefoɔ ayɛ sɛ mpoano anwea a dodoɔ nti wɔntumi nkan.
translated: And you said: I will treat you well, and make your descendants as numerous as the sand on the seashore, so many that they cannot be counted.

ref: You will live there as a foreigner, but I will be with you and bless you. I will keep my promise to your father Abraham by giving this land to you and your descendants.
text: Tena asase yi so sɛ ɔhɔhoɔ, na mɛdi w’akyi na mahyira wo, ɛfiri sɛ wo ne w’asefoɔ na mede nsase yi nyinaa bɛma mo, na mɛma ntam a mekaa w’agya Abraham no agyina.
translated: Live in this land as a foreigner, and I will be with you and bless you, for to you and your descendants I will give all these lands, and I will confirm the oath I swore to your father Abraham.

ref: Isaac started trembling and said, “Then who brought me some wild meat right before you came in? I ate it and gave him a blessing that cannot be taken back.”

translated: Then Isaac trembled very exceedingly, and said, Who? where is he that hath taken venison, and brought it me, and I have eaten of all before thou camest, and have blessed him? yea, and he shall be blessed.

ref: Cook some of that tasty food that I love so much and bring it to me. I want to eat it once more and give you my blessing before I die.”
text: na noa akɔnnɔduane sɛdeɛ mepɛ brɛ me, na menni, na me nhyira wo ansa na mawu.
translated: I have cooked the food just as I like it, but I haven't eaten, and I bless you before I die.

translated: Then the jailer committed to Joseph's care all the prisoners who were in the prison; and whatever was done there, he was the one who supervised it.

translated: Then he asked, "What shall I give you?" And Jacob said, "Give me nothing; if you will do this thing for me, I will again feed and keep your flocks."
